title: Lifecycle status enum
human_acceptance_or_explicit_next_goal_b: Next boundary recorded as RP00.P01.M05.S06 Ownership metadata; no sensitive production action approved by this subphase.

findings.title: 'implemented' lifecycle status not explicitly asserted valid
findings.details: validateHumanApprovalStatus is asserted true for 6 of the 7 shared lifecycle states (draft, hermes_validated, claude_reviewed, construction_inspected, production_ready, blocked); 'implemented' is covered only transitively via the HUMAN_APPROVAL_STATUS_VALUES deepEqual against CONTROL_PLANE_LIFECYCLE_STATES. The validator script's positive cases also omit 'implemented'. validateHumanApprovalStatus treats every enum member identically (membership check only), so functional risk is negligible.
findings.recommendation: Add validateHumanApprovalStatus("implemented") === true to the model test (and optionally the validator positive cases) for complete enum coverage.

findings.title: Verify S05 disposition status vs contractTitleAuthoritativeSubphases membership
findings.details: RP00.P01.M05.S05 is added to contractTitleAuthoritativeSubphases in the validator, while its weighted_title_disposition status is "aligned_with_weighted_ledger". The visible ternary maps set membership to expected "contract_title_authoritative"; the actual comparison is outside the shown diff context, so the diff alone cannot confirm consistency. The entry mirrors the S02-S04 pattern exactly (those subphases are committed and validating), which is strong evidence it is consistent.
findings.recommendation: Confirm scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs passes (it gates closeout) and that S05's disposition status matches the recorded S02-S04 entries. No change needed if validation passes.
